description: Regression fixture derived from the manual weitzer-org/job_tracker PR audit (review-quality-design.md §1, §7.1). Ground truth is human-verified, not GSR output. Scored against basic-mode findings from /api/review, since job_tracker's own CI (.github/workflows/gsr-review.yml) runs mode: basic exclusively — the deep-review swarm has never run on this repo (review-quality-design.md §1 correction).

must_catch.summary: containsAny (internal/ingest/filters.go) does plain substring matching for single-token needles like "tpm", so it can false-positive inside an unrelated longer word instead of requiring a word boundary the way containsAnyWord does for vpTitleKeywords.
must_catch.gap: §1 wrongly-ignored Logic finding, still live in main as of this audit.

must_catch.summary: containsAnyWord (internal/ingest/filters.go) only strips to [a-z0-9] fields, so a needle containing punctuation-adjacent characters or mixed case beyond simple lowering can miss real matches or the whole-word invariant the doc comment claims.
must_catch.gap: §1 wrongly-ignored Logic finding, still live in main as of this audit.

must_catch.summary: Store.Mutate's conflict retry loop calls time.Sleep(retryBaseDelay + rand.N(retryJitter)) on an ETag mismatch with no select on ctx.Done() — a cancelled/timed-out context does not abort the retry loop early, it just keeps sleeping and retrying until maxWriteRetries is exhausted.
must_catch.gap: §1 wrongly-ignored Logic finding, still live in main as of this audit. Same structural bug also exists in internal/status/store.go's Mutate, which this file's own comment says it mirrors.

must_catch.summary: FallbackProvider.Judge passes straight through to f.primary.Judge with no keyword/error-class handling and no doc comment explaining the choice (unlike ParseJD/ScoreCandidate, which have explicit "passes through" comments) — unclear whether Judge was meant to get the same outage-tolerant treatment as ScoreJob under NFR-4.
must_catch.gap: §1 wrongly-ignored Logic finding, still live in main as of this audit.

must_not_flag_high.summary: GSR flagged a UI race condition in saveStatus() as HIGH. Real observation, but this file is a static historical reference mockup, never served — severity should be dampened, not zero, per §4.1.
must_not_flag_high.gap: §4 correctly-ignored-but-overweighted finding — regression check is that it stays capped below HIGH/CRITICAL (or is absent), not that it disappears entirely.

must_not_flag_high.summary: GSR flagged the unbounded `while ! curl ... ; do sleep 1; done` health-check loop as HIGH ("no healthcheck timeout"). Real observation, but this is a one-off local dev-scratch script touched once and referenced by nothing else in the repo.
must_not_flag_high.gap: §4 correctly-ignored-but-overweighted finding — regression check is that it stays capped below HIGH/CRITICAL (or is absent).

must_not_flag_high.summary: GSR flagged "test failures swallowed" as HIGH — tests/e2e/run.sh's exit code is never checked before the script proceeds to "Cleaning up..." and tears the stack down regardless of pass/fail. Real observation, but this is a one-off dev-scratch script, not shipping code.
must_not_flag_high.gap: §4 correctly-ignored-but-overweighted finding — regression check is that it stays capped below HIGH/CRITICAL (or is absent).

must_resolve_cross_file.summary: PR #15 got a Logic finding claiming applyConfig (called at cmd/eval/main.go:218) is not defined in this file. False — it's defined in cmd/eval/apply.go, a brand-new file added in this same PR, valid per Go package-level scoping. §5's root cause: basic mode's useTriage=false routes runReview through the legacy file-by-file branch, so the call reviewing main.go never had apply.go's diff in the same prompt.
must_resolve_cross_file.gap: §5 cross-file false-claim, the deciding signal for whether §5.1 Tier 2 is needed once Tier 1 (aggregateChunks decoupled from useDedup) lands in Phase 2.
